description: An optimization pass that changes a program's observable behavior, silently producing wrong code for every program it transforms.
body: Failure mode: a pass applies a rewrite without fully checking its legality precondition (ignoring overflow, aliasing, undefined behavior, or side-effect order), so it 'optimizes' code in a way that changes results; the symptom appears as a mysterious application bug only at higher optimization levels. Detection signals: output differs between optimization levels; the bug disappears when the pass is disabled; differential testing finds a divergent program. Do not ship a pass not shown to be semantics-preserving. Safer fallback: gate the rewrite on a verified legality condition, validate with differential testing across a corpus including edge cases, add the failing case as a regression test, and disable the pass until it is proven.
